Requirements:
- factorial(0) == 1
- factorial(5) == 120
- factorial(-1) raises ValueError("Factorial not defined for negative numbers")
- factorial(1.5) raises TypeError("Factorial requires integer input")

Tests already exist in tests/test_factorial.py — make them pass.

title: Add factorial function with strict type checking
description:
Add a factorial function to src/prompt_bench/calculator.py.

Requirements:
- factorial(0) == 1
- factorial(5) == 120  
- factorial(20) == 2432902008176640000
- factorial(-1) raises ValueError with message containing 'negative'
- factorial(1.5) raises TypeError with message containing 'integer'
- factorial(5.0) raises TypeError (even whole-number floats must be rejected)

Tests already exist in tests/test_factorial.py — make them ALL pass.

comments.text: Cascade to prompt-bench: SKIPPED — no changed files matched any watch patterns.

title: TB-1: Issue-to-PR (Golden Path)
description: The entire loop works. A ticket goes in, a PR comes out, every layer is touched. This is the spine — everything else builds on it.

title: TB1: Intake — Poll beads for ready issues
description: Intake MCP server polls br ready --json, picks up unblocked issues, creates OTel span.

title: Edge: Agent does wrong thing for ticket — relevance check
description: Agent produces valid code that doesn't match ticket. Fix: Gate 0.5 LLM-as-judge relevance check.

title: Edge: No emergency stop
description: No way to stop all agents immediately. Fix: just emergency-stop (kill agents, pause intake).

title: Edge: Backpressure wasted (post-gate only)
description: Gates run after agent finishes. Fix: in-process type-check/test in CLAUDE.md overlay.

title: Edge: Agent hallucination — read before call
description: Agent calls tools on files it hasn't read. Fix: Read before call rule in CLAUDE.md.

title: TB Hardening: OTel Lifecycle
close_reason: OTel lifecycle fixed: span linking init from gates_span

title: TB Hardening: Low-Severity Polish
close_reason: Low-severity polish complete, remaining items accepted

title: TB1: Orchestration — dmux worktree + agent config
description: dmux creates branch + worktree from ticket metadata. Select agent persona from labels. Inject CLAUDE.md overlay.

title: TB1: Runtime — Claude Code in worktree
description: Claude Code runs in worktree with scoped CLAUDE.md. Reads ticket, modifies code, commits. Token proxy logs only (no blocking).

title: TB1: Quality Gates — DeepEval review + gitleaks scan
description: Run DeepEval LLM-as-judge review on the diff. Run gitleaks for secret scanning. Two gates for TB-1 minimal.

title: TB1: Observability — OTel traces to OpenObserve
description: OTel spans at each layer boundary. One dashboard in OpenObserve (Loop Health). Full trace visible intake → outcome.

title: TB1: Feedback Loop — single retry on gate failure
description: On gate failure: parse error, append to prompt, re-spawn agent in same worktree. Max 1 retry. Exhausted = ticket Blocked.

title: Edge: Duplicate ticket pickup — optimistic locking
description: Two polling cycles grab same ticket. Fix: claim-before-spawn with optimistic lock.

title: Edge: Crash mid-run, orphaned state — heartbeat + recover
description: Agent crashes, worktree orphaned, ticket stuck In Progress. Fix: heartbeat spans + just recover.

title: Edge: Secrets in agent context — deny list
description: Agent loads .env or secrets into context window. Fix: CLAUDE.md deny list + denied_paths config.

title: Add unit tests for core pipeline functions
description:
## Problem
pytest is a dependency but zero test files exist. The e2e run validates the
happy path but doesn't cover edge cases in individual functions.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Create tests/test_orchestration.py with ONE test: select_persona with known
   labels matches the right persona from config/agents.yaml
2. Run uv run pytest — verify the test infrastructure works
3. Expand to cover the riskiest functions:
   - test_beads_poller.py: poll_ready() with empty JSON, malformed JSON, br failure
   - test_gates.py: Gate 4 JSON parsing with fenced code blocks, bare JSON, garbage
   - test_deny_list.py: is_path_denied() with .env, .pem, normal files
   - test_heartbeat.py: find_stale_runs() with no worktree dir, empty dir, stale metadata

## Files
- NEW: tests/__init__.py
- NEW: tests/test_orchestration.py (tracer bullet)
- NEW: tests/test_beads_poller.py (expand)
- NEW: tests/test_gates.py (expand)
- NEW: tests/test_deny_list.py (expand)
- NEW: tests/test_heartbeat.py (expand)

## Exit Criteria
- At least 5 test files covering the riskiest functions
- All tests pass with uv run pytest
- Tests mock subprocess calls (don't require br or gitleaks installed)

title: TB-4: Cost-Spike-to-Pause (Budget Path)
description: Token spend is visible and controllable. Runaway agents get killed, not just logged. Requires TB-1.

title: TB4: Orchestration — cost ceiling from ticket metadata
description: Cost limit passed from ticket custom field to runtime config.

title: TB4: Runtime — token proxy with metering
description: OTel-instrumented proxy between agent and LLM API. Per-call logging with cost.

title: TB4: Gates — cost gate checks budget
description: Threshold comparison: spent vs budget. Over budget = fail + kill.

title: TB4: Observability — real-time cost dashboard
description: Token counts + model pricing → dollar amounts. Per-call, per-model, cumulative.

title: TB4: Feedback — budget exceeded alert + human approval
description: Agent killed, ticket commented with cost breakdown. Human can approve increase and restart.

title: Fix modulo() missing zero-division guard in calculator.py
description:
calculator.py modulo(a, b) does not check for b == 0 before computing a % b.
The divide() function correctly raises ZeroDivisionError when b is zero, but
modulo() is missing the same guard. Add the zero check and a test for it.

title: Validation: add power function to calculator
Make worktree base path configurable via env var
description:
## Problem
Two files hardcode WORKTREE_BASE = Path("/tmp/dev-loop/worktrees"):
- orchestration/server.py:39
- observability/heartbeat.py:32

This breaks on macOS (uses /var/folders), custom TMPDIR, or if a user
wants worktrees elsewhere. The two constants must also stay in sync.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Create a shared constant in a new file src/devloop/paths.py:
   WORKTREE_BASE = Path(os.environ.get("DEVLOOP_WORKTREE_DIR", Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "dev-loop" / "worktrees"))
2. Import from paths.py in orchestration/server.py and heartbeat.py
3. Run TB-1 with default path — verify it still works at the tempdir location
4. Run TB-1 with DEVLOOP_WORKTREE_DIR=/tmp/custom — verify it uses the custom path

## Files
- NEW: src/devloop/paths.py
- src/devloop/orchestration/server.py (line 39)
- src/devloop/observability/heartbeat.py (line 32)

## Exit Criteria
- Both files import WORKTREE_BASE from the same source
- Default behavior unchanged (uses system temp dir)
- DEVLOOP_WORKTREE_DIR env var overrides the path
- TB-1 e2e passes with both default and custom paths

title: TB-5: Cross-Repo Cascade (Multi-Project Path)
description: Changes in one repo trigger downstream work in dependent repos. Requires TB-1 passing on 2+ repos.

title: TB5: Intake — watch for merged PRs via webhook

title: TB5: Orchestration — multi-repo worktree setup
description: Create worktrees in TWO repos (source + dependent). Shared trace_id.

title: TB5: Feedback — cross-repo cascade with cycle detection
description: PR in repo A → auto-ticket for repo B. Cycle detection prevents infinite loops.

title: Improve gitleaks binary resolution
description:
## Problem
gates/server.py:39-42 hardcodes _GITLEAKS_BIN = Path.home() / ".local" / "bin" / "gitleaks"
and only falls back to bare "gitleaks" string if the hardcoded path doesn't exist.
This means: (1) shutil.which() is never tried, (2) if gitleaks is on PATH but not
at ~/.local/bin, the fallback runs bare "gitleaks" which may fail if PATH isn't
inherited, and (3) there's no clear error when gitleaks is missing entirely.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Change resolution order: shutil.which("gitleaks") first, then ~/.local/bin fallback
2. If neither found, Gate 2 should return a clear GateResult(passed=False,
   findings=[Finding(severity="critical", message="gitleaks not found...")])
   instead of a subprocess error
3. Run just tb1 — verify Gate 2 still finds gitleaks at ~/.local/bin
4. Temporarily rename gitleaks, verify the "not found" error is clear

## Files
- src/devloop/gates/server.py (lines 39-42, 338-342)

## Exit Criteria
- shutil.which() is tried first
- Missing gitleaks produces a human-readable gate failure, not a subprocess crash
- TB-1 e2e passes with gitleaks in its current location

description: ~/prompt-bench is a placeholder (1 commit, just README.md). Add a simple Python project with at least 1 test file, pyproject.toml, and README so quality gates have something to test.

title: Add timeouts to all br subprocess calls
description:
## Problem
Three files call subprocess.run() for br CLI commands with NO timeout:
- intake/beads_poller.py:65 (claim_issue) and :95 (poll_ready)
- intake/server.py:77 (_run_br helper — used by 4 tools)
- feedback/server.py:52 (_run_br helper — used by escalate_to_human)

If br hangs (corrupt DB, disk full, lock contention), the entire TB-1 pipeline blocks forever.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Add timeout=30 to intake/beads_poller.py poll_ready() ONLY
2. Run just tb1 — verify pipeline still works with the timeout
3. Expand to all 3 files (_run_br helpers + claim_issue)
4. Add subprocess.TimeoutExpired handling that returns error dicts instead of hanging

## Files
- src/devloop/intake/beads_poller.py (lines 65, 95)
- src/devloop/intake/server.py (line 77)
- src/devloop/feedback/server.py (line 52)

## Exit Criteria
- All br subprocess calls have timeout=30
- TimeoutExpired is caught and produces a clear error, not a crash
- TB-1 e2e still passes

title: Guard GateSuiteResult instantiation in retry_agent
description:
## Problem
feedback/server.py:274 does GateSuiteResult(**gate_raw) with no try/except.
If run_all_gates() returns a malformed dict (missing keys, wrong types),
the retry loop crashes with an unhandled Pydantic ValidationError instead
of reporting a structured error.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Wrap the GateSuiteResult(**gate_raw) at line 274 in try/except ValidationError
2. On failure, return RetryResult with success=False and the validation error message
3. Also guard the same pattern in pipeline.py:278 (GateSuiteResult(**gate_raw))
4. Run TB-1 with a deliberately malformed gate return to verify graceful handling

## Files
- src/devloop/feedback/server.py (line 274)
- src/devloop/feedback/pipeline.py (line 278)

## Exit Criteria
- Malformed gate output produces a RetryResult(success=False, error=...), not a crash
- TB-1 e2e still passes with normal gate output

title: Make heartbeat metadata writes atomic
description:
## Problem
observability/heartbeat.py:196-200 writes metadata JSON via direct write_text().
The heartbeat runs on a background thread (30s interval) while the main pipeline
thread also reads/writes this file during worktree setup and cleanup. If both
write simultaneously, one write is lost or the file is corrupted mid-write.

## Tracer Bullet
1. Change _touch_metadata() to write to a temp file then os.replace() (atomic on POSIX)
2. Run TB-1 — verify heartbeat metadata file is not corrupted
3. Verify find_stale_runs() still reads the file correctly

## Files
- src/devloop/observability/heartbeat.py (lines 196-200)

## Exit Criteria
- Metadata writes use write-to-temp + os.replace pattern
- No file corruption under concurrent heartbeat + pipeline access
- TB-1 e2e passes

title: Remove dead _find_session_output function
description:
## Problem
runtime/server.py:157-182 defines _find_session_output() which is never called.
The get_agent_output MCP tool (line 200+) exists but agent stdout is already
captured in AgentResult.stdout from subprocess.run. The function searches for
.claude/ session JSON files, which is a different concern (TB-6 session replay).

## Tracer Bullet
1. Delete _find_session_output() from runtime/server.py
2. Run ruff check — verify no import breakage
3. Run TB-1 — verify no runtime errors

## Files
- src/devloop/runtime/server.py (lines 157-182)

## Exit Criteria
- Function removed
- Lint passes
- TB-1 e2e unaffected

title: Validation: fix rounding in scoring module
Use --output-format json for Gate 4 review response
description:
## Problem
gates/server.py:573-603 parses Gate 4 LLM review output with fragile string
manipulation: strip markdown fences, try json.loads, fallback to brace-matching
with first { and last }. This breaks when:
- Claude wraps JSON in explanation text with nested braces
- Response has multiple JSON blocks
- Markdown fence contains language tag like \`\`\`json\n{...}\`\`\`

## Tracer Bullet
1. Add --output-format stream-json to the claude --print call for Gate 4,
   OR use --json-schema to force structured output with the expected schema:
   {"type":"object","properties":{"findings":{"type":"array"},"summary":{"type":"string"}}}
2. Parse the structured response directly — no brace-matching needed
3. Run TB-1 — verify Gate 4 produces clean JSON without the fragile parsing
4. Keep the fallback parser as a safety net but log when it's triggered

## Files
- src/devloop/gates/server.py (lines 533-543 for CLI args, 573-603 for parsing)

## Exit Criteria
- Gate 4 uses structured output (--json-schema) to get clean JSON from Claude
- Fallback parser still exists but is rarely triggered
- TB-1 e2e passes with Gate 4 producing structured review findings
